bulletins.highlights: The new snow represents the main danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The new snow must be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects.
avalancheActivity.comment: Over a wide area over a wide area 40 to 70 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 1500 m. The new snow can be released easily or naturally in all aspects. In particular in the regions exposed to heavier precipitation medium-sized dry avalanches are to be expected as the snowfall becomes more intense. The off-piste conditions are dangerous.

snowpackStructure.comment: The new snow is lying on surface hoar above the tree line. Snow profiles and stability tests have confirmed the unfavourable bonding of the snowpack. Weak layers deep in the old snowpack are difficult to recognise. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily. Individual gliding avalanches can also be released in the night or in the morning.

avalancheActivity.comment:
A lot of new snow. As a consequence of new snow and wind the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase. The snowpack will be in most cases unstable. Natural avalanches are possible over a wide area. Avalanches can penetrate deep layers reach very large size. As a consequence of the precipitation moist avalanches are possible.
Without question, it is inadvisable to engage in backcountry touring.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Somewhat older wind slabs remain for the foreseeable future prone to triggering. As a consequence of new snow and wind, further wind slabs will form.
Numerous weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

bulletins.highlights: The avalanches can occur several times in the same paths.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and warming a precarious avalanche situation will persist. New snow and wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong southwesterly wind, avalanche prone wind slabs formed. They can be released easily and reach very large size in isolated cases. This applies in particular on steep slopes above the tree line, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. The avalanche prone locations are sometimes covered with new snow and are difficult to recognise.

In many cases avalanches can be triggered in the various wind slab layers. Already many medium-sized and, in many cases, large natural dry avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches are a clear indication of a weakly bonded snowpack. Backcountry touring calls for experience in the assessment of avalanche danger and careful route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Above approximately 1000 m there is still a very large amount of snow. Over a wide area 30 to 50 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Tuesday above approximately 1500 m. As a consequence of the strong westerly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed.
Over a wide area new snow and wind slabs are lying on a weakly bonded old snowpack.

tendency.highlights: Above approximately 700 m snow will fall until late morning over a wide area. Slight increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and wind.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Considerable avalanche danger will prevail. The new snow and wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind, sometimes large wind slabs formed. Wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. This applies in particular on steep slopes, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain also at intermediate altitudes. Some small and medium-sized natural dry avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
The avalanche prone locations are sometimes covered with new snow difficult to recognise. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches are a clear indication of a weakly bonded snowpack.
Avalanches can be released in deeper layers in particular on steep shady slopes.
Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 20 to 25 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Tuesday. As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind from southerly directions, precarious wind slabs formed in particular above the tree line. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on very steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Above approximately 1000 m snow will fall until late morning in some regions. Slight increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and wind.

bulletins.highlights: The natural avalanche activity will increase.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The snow sport conditions outside marked and open pistes are critical.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular on west, north and east facing slopes more frequent medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large natural avalanches are possible during the first half of the night. In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation this applies especially. Avalanches can in many places be released in the old snowpack.

The danger exists in particular in alpine snow sports terrain. Natural avalanches and whumpfing sounds are a clear indication of a weakly bonded snowpack. Remotely triggered avalanches are probable. The avalanche prone locations are prevalent and are barely recognisable.

snowpackStructure.comment: 10 to 25 cm of snow has fallen since Tuesday. 20 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. The new snow will be deposited on a weakly bonded old snowpack. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack.

bulletins.highlights: The natural avalanche activity will increase.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The snow sport conditions outside marked and open pistes are critical.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In particular on west, north and east facing slopes more frequent medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large natural avalanches are possible during the first half of the night. In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation this applies especially. Avalanches can in many places be released in the old snowpack.

The danger exists in particular in alpine snow sports terrain. Natural avalanches and whumpfing sounds are a clear indication of a weakly bonded snowpack. Remotely triggered avalanches are probable. The avalanche prone locations are prevalent and are barely recognisable.

snowpackStructure.comment: 10 to 25 cm of snow has fallen since Tuesday. 20 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. The new snow will be deposited on a weakly bonded old snowpack. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack.

bulletins.highlights: The conditions are very precarious for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution. Natural avalanches and loose snow slides are likely to occur.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow of the last few days as well as the deep wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack. In particular on wind-loaded slopes avalanches can be released easily and reach large size in isolated cases. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.
Distinct weak layers in the lower part of the snowpack can be released even now easily by individual winter sport participants in particular on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies in particular above the tree line on steep west, north and east facing slopes. The avalanche prone locations are covered with new snow. Such avalanche prone locations are numerous and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye. The number and size of avalanche prone locations will increase with altitude.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs of the last few days are poorly bonded with the old snowpack above the tree line.
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies especially on west, north and east facing slopes. Night: Over a wide area 5 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen above approximately 1200 m.

tendency.highlights: Friday: Over a wide area 5 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall above approximately 1300 m. The avalanche danger will not decrease for the time being.

bulletins.highlights: The conditions are precarious for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution. Natural avalanches and loose snow slides are likely to occur.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow of the last few days as well as the deep wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack. In particular on wind-loaded slopes avalanches can be released easily and reach large size in isolated cases. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.
Distinct weak layers in the lower part of the snowpack can be released even now easily by individual winter sport participants in particular on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies in particular above the tree line on steep west, north and east facing slopes. The avalanche prone locations are covered with new snow. Such avalanche prone locations are numerous and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye. The number and size of avalanche prone locations will increase with altitude.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs of the last few days are poorly bonded with the old snowpack above the tree line.
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies especially on west, north and east facing slopes. Night: Over a wide area 2 to 5 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen above approximately 1200 m.

tendency.highlights: Friday: Over a wide area 5 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall above approximately 1300 m. The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The avalanche prone locations are to be found especially on steep shady slopes above the tree line. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
avalancheActivity.comment: In some regions in some localities up to 10 cm of snow will fall on Thursday. Avalanches can be released in near-ground layers by small loads. Remotely triggered and natural avalanches are possible. In addition the fresh and older wind slabs must be taken into account. The small quantity of fresh snow of Thursday and the wind slabs represent the main danger.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow of last week is lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack on shady slopes above the tree line. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.
In addition the large surface-area wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are capable of being triggered.

avalancheActivity.highlights: At intermediate and high altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes strong wind from westerly directions, avalanche prone wind slabs formed. This applies in particular on steep slopes, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain also at intermediate altitudes. The fresh wind slabs can be released by small loads. The avalanche prone locations are sometimes covered with new snow and are barely recognisable.

Some small and medium-sized natural dry avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

Avalanches can be released in deeper layers in particular on steep shady slopes. Several medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large natural dry avalanches are possible as a consequence of new snow and strong wind.

Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 15 cm of snow has fallen since Tuesday above approximately 1500 m. As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind, further wind slabs formed on Tuesday. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on very steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Above approximately 1000 m snow will fall until late morning in some regions. Slight increase in avalanche danger as a consequence of new snow and wind.

avalancheActivity.highlights: A treacherous avalanche situation will prevail. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Distinct weak layers in the lower part of the snowpack can be released easily by individual winter sport participants. In addition the fresh wind slabs are very easily triggered. Avalanches can release the weakly bonded old snow as well and reach medium size.
Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. The avalanche prone locations are sometimes covered with new snow and are therefore difficult to recognise. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.
Caution and restraint are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall.
The new snow and wind slabs will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies especially on west, north and east facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: A treacherous avalanche situation will prevail. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Distinct weak layers in the lower part of the snowpack can be released easily by individual winter sport participants. In addition the fresh wind slabs are very easily triggered. Avalanches can release the weakly bonded old snow as well and reach medium size.
Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. The avalanche prone locations are sometimes covered with new snow and are therefore difficult to recognise. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.
Caution and restraint are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall.
The new snow and wind slabs will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies especially on west, north and east facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: On the border ridges towards France and Switzerland and as you climb higher the danger points are more widespread.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and wind from southerly directions, soft wind slabs formed in the last two days.
Single winter sport participants can release avalanches in some places, including medium-sized ones. Mostly they are shallow, caution is to be exercised in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls, and on very steep slopes in particular above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are covered with new snow.
The fresh snow and especially the wind-blown snow drifts, both soft and hard, cover a thin layer of old snow. On very steep shady slopes the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach quite a large size. Slopes that have been little used this winter thus far are especially unfavourable.
Maintaining distances between individuals is recommended.
Evening and night: As a consequence of new snow and wind the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Tuesday: 5 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen above approximately 2000 m.
Wednesday: 5 to 10 cm of snow, but less in some localities, has fallen above approximately 1500 m.
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.
Several small and medium-sized slab avalanches have been released in the last few days. On Friday on steep slopes small and medium-sized avalanches occurred naturally.

tendency.highlights: Above approximately 900 m snow will fall. As a consequence of new snow and a moderate southeasterly wind, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs will form in particular in the regions exposed to precipitation. The danger of dry avalanches will already increase in the late morning.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of a moderate wind from westerly directions, wind slabs will form since Wednesday. In the course of the day the previously small wind slabs will increase in size once again. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on northwest to north to northeast facing wind-loaded slopes above approximately 2000 m.

snowpackStructure.comment: In some cases the wind slabs have bonded poorly with each other and the old snowpack. They are to be assessed critically.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh wind slabs can be released easily in some cases. This applies especially on steep shady slopes, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls above the tree line. In isolated cases avalanches are medium-sized. The avalanche prone locations are sometimes covered with new snow and are therefore difficult to recognise.

Apart from the danger of being buried, restraint should be exercised in particular in view of the danger of avalanches sweeping people along and giving rise to falls.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The fresh wind slabs will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack at elevated altitudes. The old snowpack consists of faceted crystals.

The snowpack will be generally subject to considerable local variations.

tendency.highlights: Moderate avalanche danger will prevail. Fresh wind slabs require caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. Gliding avalanches can also occur.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In some regions in some localities up to 10 cm of snow will fall on Thursday.
Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
Avalanches can be released by small loads.
Gliding avalanches can also occur at any time.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Areas with glide cracks are to be avoided as far as possible.
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh wind slabs can be released easily in some cases. This applies especially on steep shady slopes, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls above the tree line. In isolated cases avalanches are medium-sized. The avalanche prone locations are sometimes covered with new snow and are therefore difficult to recognise.

Apart from the danger of being buried, restraint should be exercised in particular in view of the danger of avalanches sweeping people along and giving rise to falls.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The fresh wind slabs will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack at elevated altitudes. The old snowpack consists of faceted crystals.

The snowpack will be generally subject to considerable local variations.

tendency.highlights: Moderate avalanche danger will prevail. Fresh wind slabs require caution.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In very isolated cases the avalanches in these regions are small and can be released by large loads.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Mostly the avalanches are small and can only be released by large loads.
Gliding avalanches can also occur.

snowpackStructure.comment: Areas with glide cracks are to be avoided. Below approximately 1000 m a little snow is lying.
